---
title: Carving wooden spoons
date: 2024-06-22
tags: [woodworking, cooking, carving, kitchen]
---

Spoon carving is the smallest, cheapest entry into woodworking I know. A hook knife, a straight knife, and a green branch of cherry or birch are enough to start, and a whole spoon can be roughed out in an evening on the back porch.

Green wood cuts like cheese and dries to a durable finish. I split the branch down the pith, draw the spoon shape on the flat face, and carve the bowl with the hook knife before shaping the handle. Working green means no sanding is needed; the knife facets themselves are the finish.

A finished spoon wants only a light coat of food-safe oil and occasional re-oiling over its life. Unlike a factory utensil it will pick up the character of the cook who uses it, and no two spoons from the same branch ever come out quite the same shape.
